steps.title: Combine the wolf findings
steps.content: The study found that black-furred wolves are more common where distemper outbreaks happen regularly and that black-furred wolves are more likely to be immune to distemper than gray-furred wolves. Together, those findings point to a connection between black fur and immunity.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content: Choice A compares gray wolves with other species, which the text never does. Choice C predicts a longer average life span, but life span is not discussed. Choice D predicts replacement of black-furred wolves by gray-furred wolves, which contradicts the evidence.

steps.title: Infer the gene connection
steps.content: The remaining choice says the gene variant that produces black fur may be linked to immunity to the distemper virus. The answer is B.
